ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "year_birth" INTEGER /* Year of birth of the customer */,
  "education" TEXT /* Education level of the customer */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  "income" REAL /* Annual income of the customer */,
  "dt_customer" TEXT /* Date when the customer registered */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "location" TEXT /* City or region where the customer resides */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "preferred_channel" TEXT /* Preferred communication channel */,
  "loyalty_points" INTEGER /* Loyalty points accumulated by the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "purchases" (
  "purchase_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purchase record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer making the purchase */,
  "mnt_wines" INTEGER /* Amount spent on wines */,
  "mnt_fruits" INTEGER /* Amount spent on fruits */,
  "mnt_meat_products" INTEGER /* Amount spent on meat products */,
  "mnt_fish_products" INTEGER /* Amount spent on fish products */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the purchase */,
  "total_amount" REAL /* Total amount spent in the purchase */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used */,
  "delivery_address" TEXT /* Delivery address for the purchase */,
  "order_status" TEXT /* Status of the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("purchase_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchases_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "web_activity" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each web activity record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer associated with the web activity */,
  "num_web_purchases" INTEGER /* Number of web purchases made by the customer */,
  "num_web_visits_month" INTEGER /* Number of web visits by the customer in the current month */,
  "activity_date" TEXT /* Date of the web activity */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the web session in minutes */,
  "pages_visited" INTEGER /* Number of pages visited during the session */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the web visit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_web_activity_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_acceptance" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer who received the campaign */,
  "accepted_cmp1" INTEGER /* Indicator if the customer accepted campaign 1 */,
  "accepted_cmp2" INTEGER /* Indicator if the customer accepted campaign 2 */,
  "accepted_cmp3" INTEGER /* Indicator if the customer accepted campaign 3 */,
  "accepted_cmp4" INTEGER /* Indicator if the customer accepted campaign 4 */,
  "accepted_cmp5" INTEGER /* Indicator if the customer accepted campaign 5 */,
  "campaign_date" TEXT /* Date the campaign was sent */,
  "campaign_type" TEXT /* Type of campaign */,
  "response_time" INTEGER /* Time taken by the customer to respond */,
  "response_channel" TEXT /* Channel through which the customer responded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_acceptance_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer segment record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer being classified */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the segment the customer belongs to */,
  "segment_description" TEXT /* Description of the segment */,
  "segment_criteria" TEXT /* Criteria used to classify customers into the segment */,
  "segment_effectiveness" TEXT /* Effectiveness of marketing strategies for the segment */,
  "segment_trend" TEXT /* Trend of the segment over time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segments_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
